meta.title: Magic Resume — the AI resume workbench
meta.description: A resume workbench where AI proposes edits in place and you decide. Guided drafting, role-match analysis, mock interviews — open-source core, cloud sync.

hero.title: The resume workbench built for your target role
hero.subtitle: AI proposes edits right on the page — accept what fits, skip the rest. Every change syncs to all your devices, instantly.

bento.title: Six modes. One workbench.
bento.subtitle: Everything between a blank page and a sent application. Try it below — the skills run right on this page, with mock data.

welcome.title: How shall we polish your resume today?
welcome.sub: Describe what you need — I'll pick the right skill.
welcome.placeholder: Describe a task, or type / to call a skill…

chat.mockReply: This is a demo playground, so I'll keep your data untouched. In the real app I'd pick the right skill and run it on your resume — type / to try one →

chips.ask: Optimize my projects, highlight quantified impact
chips.label: Optimize my projects

chips.ask: Run a competitiveness check on my resume
chips.label: Run a competitiveness check

optimize.reply: Before rewriting, let's lock the target role so every edit aims the right way. Fill in the target role:
optimize.jdPh: Paste the job description — AI will steer the rewrite…
optimize.applied: Done — rewrote one bullet toward the target JD. Applied on the canvas →

analyze.result: Overall competitiveness 86/100 — strongest signal: quantified impact. Two gaps flagged on the canvas →

translate.reply: Translated to Chinese — layout intact. See the canvas →

interview.reply: Reading your resume… let's start with the Google internship:
interview.question: “You cut memory leaks by 25% — how did you find them, and how did you verify the fix?”
interview.hint: The related bullet is highlighted on the canvas →

modes.title: Create
modes.desc: Guided drafts from a blank page

workflow.title: AI proposes. You decide.
workflow.subtitle: No black-box rewrites. Every change is a reasoned proposal — red for cuts, green for adds, fully traceable — and nothing lands until you say so.
points: Edits are proposed in place, never applied silently
Each proposal comes with a reason
Skip anything; the resume stays yours

items.after: Led a 5-engineer frontend team, shipping 3 products in a year.
Cut page load time 42%, lifting conversion 18%.
Full-stack engineer who ships fast and sweats the details.

sync.title: Edit once. Everywhere at once.
sync.subtitle: Cloud sync is on by default — every word you change at your desk is already on your phone. Full version history: roll back or export anytime.
points: Self-host the open-source core, or use the cloud

openSource.title: Built in the open
openSource.desc: The Magic Resume core is MIT-licensed and built with its community. Read the code, file an issue, or star the repo.

items.a: The open-source edition is free forever and self-hostable. The cloud edition adds sync and built-in AI with a generous free tier — upgrade when you need more.
Sync is encrypted in transit and at rest, the data stays yours, and you can export everything anytime. Want fully offline? Self-host the open-source edition.

items.q: Will AI change my resume without asking?
items.a: Never. Every edit arrives as a proposal — red cuts, green adds, with a reason — and nothing applies until you accept it.
One-click ATS-friendly PDF, plus JSON in and out for backup and migration.

items.q: How is it different from other resume builders?
items.a: It's not a form filler — it's a workbench that knows your target role: create, optimize, analyze, translate and rehearse, all on one canvas.

cta.title: From blank page to sent application
cta.subtitle: Build a resume that knows the role you're aiming for.
cta.note: Open-source core · Cloud sync · Export anytime

footer.brandSubtitle: The AI resume workbench that makes a job search feel simple.
